rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 29, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF JOSE CERON, MONICA MEDINA, WENDY ENRIQUEZ, DERIK ENDERSON, JUDY JUDKINS Notice Of Motion To Strike GRANTED, in Part and DENIED, in part. Request for Judicial notice is granted. No opposition filed to the request for judicial notice. The Court notes that Cross-Complainant failed to provide the Court with a courtesy copy of the opposition. (San Francisco Local Rule, Rule 2.7B.) Failure to comply with this rule in the future will result in the Motion being treated as Unopposed. It also appears to the Court that Cross-Complainant used font Courier Std., size 10. Papers that do not comply with CRC Rules 2.104 and 2.105 in the future will be stricken by the Court. Motion to Strike the 1st and 23rd Causes of Action as to Ceron and Medina is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Cross-Complainant stipulates to remove part of the allegations re: storing of personal belongings underneath the staircase; As to the remaining allegations, the settlement agreement does not address or prohibit the alleged conduct. Motion to Strike the 2nd and 3rd Causes of Action is DENIED. Cross-Complainant alleges new claims not barred by Res Judicata. Motion to Strike the 20th Cause of Action is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Cross-Complainant alleges the same theory and allegations as in the 3rd Cause of Action. Motion to Strike the 9th Cause of Action is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 33 is GRANTED without leave to amend. Motion to Strike Paragraph 34 is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 35 is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 36 is GRANTED with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant to clarify whether Cross-Defendants are storing a broken oven by the garbage cans or threw out a broken oven by the garbage cans in 2016. Motion to Strike Paragraph 37 is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 188 is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 189 is GRANTED without leave to amend. Motion to Strike Paragraph 191 is DENIED. Motion to Strike Paragraph 192 is DENIED. Motion to strike punitive damages is DENIED. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to claim for attorney's fees, e.g. statute or binding contract. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHUN KUM EXTER VS. SAM HOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. THOMAS MILLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 29, 2019 line 7. PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION Ntc Of Mtn For Entry Of Judgment CONTINUED to June 5, 2019. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of all pages of the moving papers to Department 501 no later than May 30, 2019 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Specifically, Plaintiff failed to attach all pages of the settlement agreement. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ELAN BERNSTEIN VS. BRUCE LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 29, 2019 line 9. DEFENDANT BRUCE LIU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The Court finds that under these circumstances, a unit without a certificate of occupancy is not a comparable unit under Rent Ordinance 37.9(a)(8)(iv). Defendant to file an answer within 5 days. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 371 BROADWAY STREET, LLC VS. NATHAN CHARTIER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 29, 2019 line 10. PLAINTIFF 371 BROADWAY STREET, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Request for Production, Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories by May 31, 2019. Sanctions granted in the amount of $60 for costs. The Court further inquires into how Counsel attempted to resolve the discovery matter informally considering Counsel could not get in contact with Defendant. = (501/CFH)
